summary: Tested Living Circle MVP (FastAPI backend + Expo/RN frontend). Backend: 17/17 pytest tests pass (auth, profile, discover w/ filters, swipe/match, messages, block/report, seed, compatibility). Frontend: complete onboarding → discover → filter → swipe → tab nav flow works end-to-end with no JS errors. All required testIDs present. Two minor issues found: (1) filter sheet sends 'Female'/'Male' capitalized but seed data stores gender lowercase, so the gender filter returns 0 results; (2) screen-cta testID is duplicated across mounted stack screens (Expo Router keeps prior screens in DOM) — tests still pass but selectors should use .last. No React Native deprecated packages found (uses expo-haptics, expo-linear-gradient — all current).

minor.issue: Case-sensitive equality with seed data: seed profiles store gender as 'male'/'female' (lowercase) but onboarding chips and filter UI send 'Female'/'Male' (capitalized). Filtering with the UI value returns zero seeded results. Either normalize on read/write or use case-insensitive regex like locality.

ui_bugs.issue: testID='screen-cta' is rendered on every onboarding step; because Expo Router web stack keeps prior screens mounted, multiple elements with the same testID exist concurrently. Use unique testIDs per screen (e.g. 'profile-cta', 'details-cta') or ensure only the top screen has the active testID.
Filter values for gender ('Female'/'Male') do not match seed gender values ('female'/'male') — applying gender filter returns empty.

issues: When age/org/budget are missing, card still renders trailing punctuation e.g. 'TEST_Disc, ' (empty age) and '₹–'. Guard rendering with optional chaining or skip rendering when fields are empty.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Triggered in discover.tsx card and likely other components. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/discover.tsx lines ~441-444 and ~488-490.

action_items: Lowercase or case-normalize the gender filter on backend (e.g. q['gender'] = {'$regex': f'^{gender}$', '$options': 'i'}) OR send lowercase gender values from the filter sheet and onboarding.
Replace deprecated React Native Web `shadow*` style props with `boxShadow` to silence console warnings.
Make screen-cta testID unique per onboarding step (profile-cta, details-cta, lifestyle-cta, listing-cta) to avoid duplicate testIDs from stacked screens kept in DOM by Expo Router.
Discover card: guard rendering of age/org/budget so users with empty values don't render dangling punctuation. (Cosmetic; also clean up TEST_* seed-test users created during backend testing.)
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 559 lines — close to the 700-line threshold but acceptable; consider splitting into routers if it grows.
Auth uses raw user_id as a bearer token (no expiry). Acceptable for MVP/mock-OTP but should be flagged for productionization.
Discover excludes blocked & swiped properly. Compatibility computed only over answered lifestyle questions (defaults to 50 when none) — correct.
All responses correctly exclude MongoDB _id via projection {'_id': 0}.
seed_data_creation: Backend pytest created several TEST_-prefixed dummy profiles (via /auth/verify-otp + /profiles/me) which now appear in /profiles/discover for new users. Consider cleaning the test_database 'profiles' collection of TEST_* users before user demos.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_living_circle.py is the regression source-of-truth (17 tests). For Expo Router web preview, multiple stack screens stay mounted — use page.locator('[data-testid=...]').last for elements that share testIDs across screens (e.g. screen-cta).
rca_of_issue: Gender filter bug RCA: onboarding profile chips define GENDERS = ['Female','Male','Non-binary','Other'] (capitalized) while seed data in server.py SEED_NAMES uses 'male'/'female' (lowercase). The /profiles/discover query uses exact equality q['gender'] = gender, so capitalized-vs-lowercase mismatch returns 0 seed profiles. Fix either by normalizing on read (regex) or by aligning seed values with UI values.
